purpose: Prove kernel (c) in cublaslt_det_microbench.py (the verbatim-vendored matmul_persistent) is byte-identical to the LIVE vLLM 0.22.0 batch_invariant kernel, so the microbench's Triton-invariant numbers are exactly vLLM's kernel and not a faithful-copy approximation.
method: Imported vllm.model_executor.layers.batch_invariant.matmul_persistent (live) and cublaslt_det_microbench._matmul_persistent_vendored (this script's copy) in the SAME process (same triton 3.6.0), ran both on 8 representative gemma-4-E4B-it decode shapes at M=1 bf16, asserted torch.equal.
conclusion: Vendored kernel == live vLLM 0.22.0 kernel, bit-for-bit, on all 8 shapes (max_abs=0). Kernel (c) timings in the microbench are vLLM's actual batch-invariant kernel.
